key_insight: This survey frames large language models as flexible intermediaries for vehicle routing problems, enabling natural-language modeling, heuristic generation, and tool-driven coordination across VRP variants.
engineering_relevance: The work is relevant for logistics, transportation scheduling, and industrial automation because it suggests how LLMs can help translate real operational constraints into solvable routing workflows.
method: Translate natural-language routing requirements into constraints and modeling code
Generate heuristics, operators, or full route plans for VRP variants
Coordinate tool calls, multi-agent workflows, and neural solver integration
evidence_signal: The survey grounds its discussion with standard benchmarks, real or near-real operational datasets, LLM-oriented evaluation frameworks, and two comparative experiments.
watch_item: LLM outputs for routing are still sensitive to constraint ambiguity and tool integration errors, so generated plans should be checked against formal optimization requirements.
paper_title: Vehicle Routing Problem Meets Large Language Models: An Overview and Perspectives
